tasks.title: Create additive seam and preserve matrix
tasks.description: Files: update `.codex/uw/context/product-spec.md`, `.codex/uw/context/implementation-plan.md`, `.codex/uw/context/task-breakdown.json`, and `.codex/uw/context/user-journeys.json` so every stream names the current seam it extends, the non-goals it preserves, and the minimal new modules allowed.
acceptance_criteria: Every epic and task names the current seam it extends.
Preserve and non-goal rules are explicit in UW artifacts.
No early-phase task implies a second engine or framework.
test_cases: Run the UW validator and confirm the planning artifacts are internally consistent.
Review all epic and task descriptions for explicit seam references.
tasks.expected_output: Validated additive seam matrix embedded in UW planning artifacts
definition_of_done: UW planning artifacts are rewritten around additive control-plane work.
No task requires rewriting workflow, ledger, compliance, or adapter cores.
The task graph remains deterministic and acyclic.

tier.justification: Cross-artifact planning task with moderate scope and low ambiguity.

tasks.title: Define compatibility gate matrix
tasks.description: Files: extend `.codex/uw/context/product-spec.md` and `.codex/uw/context/implementation-plan.md` with the concrete compatibility contract for OpenAPI, SDKs, widget, CLI, and migration rehearsal; note expected repo touchpoints in CI and release scripts.

tasks.expected_output: Compatibility gate matrix and release-safety contract
definition_of_done: Compatibility gates are named in planning artifacts.
The release plan references those gates.
The task graph uses the gates as dependencies for rollout and certification work.

tier.justification: Moderate planning work that drives later QA and release tasks.

tasks.title: Design partner and capability registry schema
tasks.description: Files: add new additive migrations under `migrations/` for partners, capabilities, health state, rollout scope, and approval references; define a shared partner taxonomy that covers rails, PSPs, compliance vendors, LPs, custodians, Travel Rule networks, and governed DeFi connectors; reference current admin and config services instead of creating a parallel registry framework.
acceptance_criteria: The schema can model rails, PSPs, compliance vendors, LPs, custodians, and DeFi partners.
Capabilities, rollout scope, and health signals are first-class records instead of embedded partner flags.
Schema changes are additive and backfillable.
Backfill preserves current admin/config identity and avoids a second registry runtime.
test_cases: Validate migration naming and dependency ordering in the plan.
Check that the schema supports all partner classes listed in the product spec.
Confirm rollout-scope and health-state records stay additive instead of collapsing into a second registry runtime.
tasks.expected_output: Partner and capability registry schema plan with shared partner taxonomy and additive rollout-health slices
definition_of_done: Registry entities are reflected in the product spec data model.
Capability and rollout-scope records are reusable across current admin and config seams.
Task dependencies remain acyclic.
No second registry framework is introduced.

tier.justification: Core schema planning with multiple partner classes and governance semantics.
